Rated PG September 1 - The Cat in the Hat (2003). Run Time: 1h 22 min. Rated PG September 4 - Ratatouille (2007). Run Time 1h 51 min. Rated G.

events.title: Yoga Storytime with Appleseed Yoga
events.description: ​Join us for an interactive, movement-based program where we read stories and make them come alive with our bodies! Appleseed Yoga for Kids will deliver inspiring stories that get the whole family moving. Class begins and ends with song and breathwork and is filled with pages of creativity and activity. Kids and families can sit quietly and listen, do partial poses or make up their own, following our lead. It's ALL yoga! No experience, mats, or materials required. Drop-in with limited space. All

events.title: Learn Not to Burn: Fire Safety for Children

events.title: Under the Sea BINGO

G) August 27 - Muppet Treasure Island (1996, 99 min. G) For children ages 6 and up. Drop in. No registration is required. Daycares and Camps, please call 416-393-7720 to inquire.

events.title: Fictional Character Bingo and TD Summer Reading Club Draw

Maybe you can guess who they are too! Prizes will be given out for Bingo winners! Also, if you've completed the TD Summer Reading Club challenge of finishing 12 books, we'll be doing a draw for special prizes at the end of the program. Drop-in!

events.title: Wonder Workshop - Pollution Challenge
events.description: Do you ever think about the impact of one little person or one tiny thing on the planet? When one pollutant becomes many, the planet hurts. But when one person becomes many, positive change can happen. In this workshop, you will design and build your own creature or machine that will help keep our precious planet clean! events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Sew Cool Kids:  Cross-Stitch
events.description: This fun and creative 4 week program introduces children aged 6 - 12 to the art of cross-stitching! Kids will learn the basic stitches, how to follow simple patterns, and how to use colorful threads to bring a design to life.  By the end of the program, each child will have completed a bright and cheerful stitched picture to proudly take home and display. Registering will register your child for all 4 sessions.

events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.description: Explore different STEM-themed activities every Sunday. Drop-in, no registration required. Space is limited. Access and use of equipment will be on a first come, first serve basis. Suitable for ages 6-12.

From building simple robots and experimenting with Snap Circuits to creative design challenges and more, there’s always something new to discover. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

Creative Construction is a fun, hands on program where kids ages 4–12 can use their imagination and problem-solving skills with a variety of building materials, including Feltro, LEGO, Jumbo LEGO, building straws, and Keva planks. Drop-in.  For ages 4-12.

events.title: Club de lecture de bande dessinée
events.description: *English text follows* Pratiquez votre français pendant les vancances d'été ! Chaque mois, lisez une nouvelle bande dessinée en français de votre choix en lien avec le thème proposé, et venez nous en parler. Bien sûr, nous nous amuserons aussi avec diverses activités. Le thème du mois de juillet est la magie . Choisissez un livre parmi ceux mentionnés ci-dessous ou tout autre ouvrage correspondant au thème . Les sorcières de Brooklyn de Sophie Escabasse L'île des mythes de Anaïs Halard

events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. School-aged participants (6-12) will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the palaeontological aspects that underpin them.  Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and—of course—dinosaurs! events.title: Stuffy Sleepover
events.description: Put on your pyjamas, pack up your stuffy, and visit the Riverdale branch for a Stuffy Sleepover! Listen to stories, participate in a fun craft and then leave your stuffy to spend the night at the library. For children ages 3 and up, their parents/caregivers and their stuffed animal friends. Only the stuffies will be sleeping over. Pick up your stuffy after the sleepover and learn how your friend spent the night. Drop in. No registration is required.

events.title: Family Movie: Zootopia 2
events.description: Join our Friday afternoon Family Movies ! In the sequel to 2016's Zootopia , newly-promoted detectives Judy Hopps and Nick Wilde investigate a mysterious pit viper named Gary De'Snake. Defying police orders, the duo goes on the run through new city sectors, uncovering a massive conspiracy involving the displaced reptile population and the city's corrupt founding families.

Then use your imagination to design and build your own mock butterfly garden station. Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book.  For kids ages 6 to 10. Space is limited. This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. Join us for an introductory lesson and learn about the most common tools. We'll explore the workspace and enhance images while investigating tools such as crop, levels, colour balance and more. events.title: Let’s Animate!
events.description: Learn the basics of animation and see your imagination in motion. With personal devices or traditional pencil and paper, use the principles of animation to bring your characters to life. No experience is necessary but curiosity and a willingness to have fun and be creative are required! This workshop will be guided by Enzo Avolio who has animated for both Disney and Warner Bros. TV and has been in animation and the arts for over 30 years. Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: Let's Learn About Computer Hardware
events.description: This hands-on workshop series introduces the process of building a gaming desktop, from selecting components to installing an operating system. Participants will disassemble a desktop to learn about internal hardware and basic troubleshooting. The series also covers OS selection and installation, along with essential cybersecurity tips using open-source tools. This workshop is run by Free Geek Toronto.

events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Art and Game Workshop
events.description: This program is part of Youth service in a welcoming place for teens in the summer.  Do you love Games and crafts? Bring your creativity and imagination to our workshop for a fun day at the library. Workshops include DIY button making, bookmark, phone charms, board games, and more! Registration is required. Material will be provided. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act, sections 2

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Inf

events.title: September Acrylic Paint Night
events.description: Join this casual painting session and create your own masterpiece! Theme: Fall Supplies will be provided, including canvas boards, acrylic paints and brushes Please note that this is not an art class. Participants will paint by watching and following a step-by-step tutorial video together. Registration is required. Please register online. Visit or call the branch if you need help with registering. events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. June 11: The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014, 14A) June 18: Best in Show (2000, PG) June 25: The Big Lebowski (1998, 14A) July 2: Born on the 4th of July (1989, 18A) July 9: American Made (2017, 14A) July 16: Jerry Maguire (1996, 14A) July 23: Rain Man (1988, 14A) July 30: Top Gun: Maverick (2022, PG) All are welcome. events.title: Jigsaw Puzzle Social & Swap
events.description: Join us for an afternoon of communal puzzling! Bring your own puzzle or try one of the ones provided. Puzzles will be suitable for all ages. events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Saturday Knit & Crochet Circle
events.description: Want to learn how to knit or crochet with a fun group of people? Looking for help with your project? You can also share your projects with others, teach or learn a technique, and enjoy great conversation. All levels of expertise are welcome. Bring your own yarn, needles, and crochet hooks. All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.description: Come and join us on the third Tuesday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Taxation and Government Benefits
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop will provide guidance on tax filing, understanding available benefits and credits and its eligibility requirements, and how to access applicable government financial supports. Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Creating Parts With Machinery This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Tuesday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. *With the exception of June, where we will meet on the 4th Tuesday. This year we are reading:  June 23 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 15 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 17 -  Small Things Like These by Claire Keegan

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: LivingWorks safeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks safeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks safeTALK certificat

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service.
